description:
You are assisting a data-analytics team that versions its internal tools with semantic version numbers (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).  
The project folder already exists:

  /home/user/data_project  
    ├── version.txt            # contains exactly one line with the current version string  
    └── CHANGELOG.md           # a markdown changelog, newest version entry always on top  

Current state of the two files:

1. /home/user/data_project/version.txt  
   The file contains a single line, ending with a Unix newline (`\n`):  
   1.3.5

2. /home/user/data_project/CHANGELOG.md  

   # Changelog  
   
   ## [1.3.5] - 2023-06-30  
   - Added median calculation for quarterly_sales.csv  

Task
-----
The analysts have just fixed an error in the quarterly_sales.csv aggregation step and need to bump the **patch** version.

You must perform ALL of the following with a **single compound shell command** (you may chain several utilities with `&&`, `;`, pipes, subshells, etc., but the entire job must be issued as one single line that is entered only once):

A. Update /home/user/data_project/version.txt so that it contains exactly the new version string  
   1.3.6  
   followed by a single trailing newline. No extra spaces or characters.

B. Prepend a new section at the very top of /home/user/data_project/CHANGELOG.md, directly under the first line `# Changelog`, in the format shown below (three lines, each terminated by `\n`). The date is fixed and MUST match exactly.

   ## [1.3.6] - 2023-07-10  
   - Fixed average calculation bug in quarterly_sales.csv  

   After this insertion, the previous content must remain unmodified and immediately follow the new block, so the resulting CHANGELOG.md must look exactly like:

   # Changelog  
   
   ## [1.3.6] - 2023-07-10  
   - Fixed average calculation bug in quarterly_sales.csv  
   
   ## [1.3.5] - 2023-06-30  
   - Added median calculation for quarterly_sales.csv  

C. Create a plain-text verification record called /home/user/data_project/bump_log.txt with precisely the two lines below (each line terminated by `\n`):

   Old version: 1.3.5  
   New version: 1.3.6  

Important constraints
---------------------
1. You must accomplish everything with **one single terminal command**.  
2. Do not manually edit files with an interactive editor like `nano` or `vim`; rely on standard shell utilities (e.g., `sed`, `echo`, `printf`, `tee`, etc.).  
3. File permissions must remain unchanged (the default permissions of the existing files are fine).  
4. The automated grader will assert that:  
   • /home/user/data_project/version.txt contains exactly `1.3.6\n`  
   • /home/user/data_project/CHANGELOG.md is byte-for-byte identical to the specification above  
   • /home/user/data_project/bump_log.txt exists and contains the two exact lines listed in step C.

When you are done, display the contents of the three files in the terminal so the human reviewer can see the new versions and changelog.
truth:
Initial filesystem snapshot (before the agent starts):

/home/user/data_project  
├── version.txt   (contents: "1.3.5\n")  
└── CHANGELOG.md  (contents exactly:)  
# Changelog\n
\n
## [1.3.5] - 2023-06-30\n
- Added median calculation for quarterly_sales.csv\n

No other files are present in /home/user/data_project.

Expected filesystem snapshot after successful completion:

/home/user/data_project  
├── version.txt   (contents: "1.3.6\n")  
├── CHANGELOG.md  (contents exactly:)  
# Changelog\n
\n
## [1.3.6] - 2023-07-10\n
- Fixed average calculation bug in quarterly_sales.csv\n
\n
## [1.3.5] - 2023-06-30\n
- Added median calculation for quarterly_sales.csv\n
├── bump_log.txt  (contents exactly:)  
Old version: 1.3.5\n
New version: 1.3.6\n

The grader will compare files byte-for-byte against the above truth data.
